question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $70,000 on April 20?

fragility_points: Potential downside volatility over a six-day horizon.
Minor operational ambiguity from website-candle resolution wording rather than explicit API language.
key_assumptions: BTC remains in the current 70k-plus trading regime through the April 20 noon ET settlement minute.
Binance continues to function normally as the governing BTC/USDT price source.
No major macro or crypto shock forces a regime break below 70k before settlement.
main_logical_chain: The governing contract asks only whether Binance BTC/USDT closes above 70k on the 12:00 ET one-minute candle on April 20.
Current Binance price and recent regime put BTC comfortably above the threshold with a short time horizon remaining.
But the narrow one-minute settlement condition makes the event less robust than the market price suggests.
Therefore Yes is favored, but less strongly than the market's 84.5% baseline.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is more likely than not because BTC is currently well above 70k on Binance and the horizon is short, but the market overstates confidence because settlement depends on a single 12:00 ET one-minute close.
quote_anchors: Yes requires the final Close of the Binance BTC/USDT 1m candle at 12:00 ET on April 20 to be higher than 70,000.
payload.source_quality_view: Good direct evidence for a medium-difficulty price market: Polymarket rules define settlement and Binance is the named source, but evidence independence is only medium because both core sources are tightly linked to the same contract/venue.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact future minute, not on a daily close or average price.
Only 15 of the last 30 Binance daily closes sampled were above 70k, which is materially below the market-implied 84.5%.
BTC volatility is large enough that a several-thousand-dollar move over six days is structurally plausible.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT spot was about 74.25k during research, leaving roughly a 4.25k cushion above the threshold.
Six of the most recent seven Binance daily closes sampled were above 70k.
Only six days remain until settlement, limiting the time for a full regime reversal.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the contract depends on the April 20, 2026 12:00 ET one-minute Binance BTC/USDT close, operationally equivalent to about 16:00 UTC under EDT.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance website presentation could matter in edge cases differently from API retrieval.
How stable the 70k regime remains through US-hours trading into settlement.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC loses the 70k regime before settlement or if contract-specific timing/source interpretation risk proves larger than assumed; I would raise it if BTC keeps holding several thousand dollars above 70k into the weekend with lower downside volatility.

fragility_points: Exchange-specific pricing creates venue risk not captured by broader BTC narratives.
Probability should fall quickly if BTC compresses back toward 71k-70k before resolution.
key_assumptions: No major macro or crypto-specific shock forces a >5% downside move before settlement.
Binance trading conditions remain normal enough that the exchange-specific print is not unusually distorted.
Recent trading range remains informative over the next six days.
main_logical_chain: Contract settles on Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close on April 20.
Current Binance spot is materially above 70k, creating a meaningful cushion.
Therefore the residual question is whether a downside catalyst before settlement can overcome that cushion.
No identified sourced catalyst currently looks strong enough on its own to make No the base case.
So Yes remains more likely, but slightly less likely than the market implies because timestamp and venue fragility still matter.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is already materially above 70k on Binance, so Yes remains the base case unless a downside shock or Binance-specific dislocation hits before the April 20 noon ET settlement minute.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
Binance ticker check during the run returned BTCUSDT around 74,049.50.
payload.source_quality_view: Source quality is good: Polymarket gives clear authoritative settlement rules, Binance API gives direct exchange pricing, and CoinGecko adds secondary range context; independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low on mechanics but not zero on venue-specific execution risk.
strongest_disconfirmers: This is a single-minute, single-exchange settlement, so a late shock or venue-specific dislocation could resolve No without a durable trend break.
A macro-led risk-off move or liquidation cascade could erase the current cushion quickly.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ticker check showed BTCUSDT around 74049.50, roughly 5.8% above the threshold.
Recent Binance 1m klines were clustered around 74.0k-74.3k rather than barely above 70k.
CoinGecko 7-day data places 70k inside the recent range rather than far above market.
payload.timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract resolves on one Binance minute at noon ET on April 20, making late downside catalysts far more important than generic medium-term bullishness.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether any unobserved macro calendar item before April 20 carries enough downside information value to move BTC by more than the current cushion.
Whether Binance-specific microstructure stress could emerge near the event minute.
payload.what_would_change_view: A move back toward 71k-70k with rising volatility, a macro shock, or Binance operational stress would make me materially less confident in Yes.

fragility_points: Binance-specific pricing or operational anomaly near the resolving minute.
Unobserved realized volatility closer to settlement could make the noon close more fragile than the current pass suggests.
key_assumptions: BTC remains in roughly the current mid-70k trading regime through April 20.
No macro or crypto-specific shock produces a drawdown greater than about 6% into the resolving minute.
Binance BTC/USDT remains a reliable and representative settlement venue at noon ET.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 84.5% prior and take it seriously because short-dated threshold markets often encode useful strike-distance information.
Verify the governing rules and exact settlement source: Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute candle at 12:00 PM ET on April 20.
Check the exact venue's current price and recent context; Binance spot is comfortably above 70k and recent closes are above the threshold.
Apply a modest discount for minute-specific, venue-specific settlement risk and normal BTC volatility.
Net result: agree with the market's direction but estimate slightly lower at 82%.
payload.main_thesis: The market is mostly efficient: Binance BTC/USDT is currently comfortably above 70k, so Yes should be favored, but the exact noon-ET one-minute-close mechanic merits a small discount versus the market's mid/high-80s confidence.
quote_anchors: Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone
final "Close" price higher than the price specified
payload.source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is strong because Binance is the named settlement venue and Polymarket supplies the contract rules; independence is only medium because the core evidence is mechanically tied to the settlement source, with CoinGecko serving as a contextual cross-check.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one exact Binance 1-minute close at noon ET, so a single volatile minute can flip the result.
A roughly 6% drawdown from current spot would be enough to settle No and is plausible over six days in BTC.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot check showed BTCUSDT around 74,258, materially above the 70,000 threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes in the sampled window were all above 70,000.
Polymarket's own ladder was internally coherent, implying traders already price downside without rejecting a strong Yes lean.
payload.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves in six days on an exact minute-close, and the ET-to-UTC conversion was verified as 2026-04-20 16:00 UTC.
unresolved_ambiguities: How volatile the exact noon ET minute is likely to be on April 20.
Whether any Binance UI/data-surface quirks could matter for practical settlement interpretation.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move lower if BTC falls back toward the low-71k/high-70k area, if Binance-specific risks emerge, or if closer-to-resolution minute-level checks show materially higher noon-close fragility.

fragility_points: A sharp downside move into the exact noon ET minute could fail the contract even if the broader weekly thesis stays bullish.
Venue-specific anomalies on Binance matter more than usual because no cross-exchange averaging is allowed.
The market may be underpricing uncertainty rather than misreading direction.
key_assumptions: BTC remains in an above-70k regime through the April 20 noon ET observation window.
No major risk-off move erases the current roughly 6% buffer before the exact settlement minute.
Binance remains operationally reliable without a settlement-relevant print anomaly.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves from the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute final close on April 20.
Current Binance price context places BTC materially above the 70000 threshold.
Recent completed daily closes above 70000 support a Yes base case.
But short-horizon crypto volatility and timestamp-specific settlement mechanics make the market's 84.5% confidence look slightly high.
Therefore Yes remains favored, but at a discounted confidence estimate of 79%.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is favored because Binance BTC/USDT is currently comfortably above 70000, but the market is slightly overconfident for a single-minute timestamp-specific contract.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than ... 70000.
Current market-implied probability is 84.5% from the assignment price of 0.845.
payload.source_quality_view: Low source-of-truth ambiguity and strong directness from contract rules plus Binance data, but only medium independence because the evidence set is concentrated on the same contract/venue stack.
strongest_disconfirmers: Recent BTC daily ranges on Binance were still large enough that a sub-70k noon print remains plausible.
This contract settles on one exact one-minute close, so spot distance can overstate confidence.
Binance-only settlement creates venue-specific dislocation risk.
strongest_supports: Binance is the named settlement venue and BTC/USDT the named pair.
Spot at retrieval time was about 74258 on Binance, well above 70000.
Recent sampled daily Binance closes were all above 70000.
payload.timing_relevance: Timing is central because the market settles on one exact 12:00 ET one-minute close on April 20, not on current spot or daily close.
unresolved_ambiguities: No stronger independent causal catalyst review was obtained beyond direct price and contract checks.
Exact Binance source-surface behavior at settlement time still carries some operational uncertainty.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would cut the estimate if BTC trades persistently near 71k/70k before April 20 or if Binance shows venue-specific anomalies; I would move closer to market if BTC stays stably above roughly 72k-73k into the final 24-48 hours.

fragility_points: The disagreement versus market is modest; if BTC grinds higher or volatility compresses, the edge disappears.
Evidence set is intentionally lightweight and does not include a full realized/implied volatility study.
key_assumptions: Traders may underweight the difference between BTC currently trading above 70k and the exact Binance noon ET settlement close being above 70k.
A roughly 5-6% BTC drawdown over six days remains plausible enough to keep No live.
main_logical_chain: Read the contract mechanics and confirm the governing source of truth is Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 PM ET on April 20.
Check current BTC level on Binance and independent contextual source; both place BTC around 74.2k-74.3k.
Translate the question into required path risk: BTC needs only about a 5.7% decline by the exact settlement minute to fail.
Conclude Yes is still favored, but the market may be slightly overconfident because narrow timing risk remains material.
payload.main_thesis: The market is directionally right that Yes is favored, but may be modestly overconfident because the contract settles on one exact Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at noon ET, leaving meaningful short-horizon path risk.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.
payload.source_quality_view: High for rules and current spot level, medium for independence because price checks reflect related market data surfaces.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC already has a meaningful cushion above 70k with only six days remaining, so an 84.5%-85% Yes price is not obviously too high.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was 74238, already above the threshold by about 5.7%.
Polymarket rules clearly define a narrow venue-and-minute-specific settlement condition rather than a broad BTC thesis.
CoinGecko independently confirmed BTC was in the mid-74k area.
payload.timing_relevance: High: this contract resolves on a single 12:00 PM ET Binance 1-minute close, which maps to 16:00 UTC on April 20, 2026.
unresolved_ambiguities: No major source-of-truth ambiguity remains after the rules check.
The remaining uncertainty is market-path uncertainty rather than rule ambiguity.
payload.what_would_change_view: I would move toward or above market if BTC builds a larger cushion above 75.5k-76k and volatility compresses; I would move lower if BTC quickly loses the mid-74k area or downside stress rises into the settlement window.
